categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ أَصْحَابِ ٱلْكَهْفِ
categories.nameEn: Words from the story of the People of the Cave

items.verse: نَّحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ وَزِدْنَـٰهُمْ هُدًۭى
items.verseEn: It is We who relate to you, [O Muḥammad], their story in truth. Indeed, they were youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

items.verse: وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا۟ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَن نَّدْعُوَا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَـٰهًۭا ۖ لَّقَدْ قُلْنَآ إِذًۭا شَطَطًا
items.verseEn: And We bound [i.e., made firm] their hearts when they stood up and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never will we invoke besides Him any deity. We would have certainly spoken, then, an excessive transgression.

items.verse: وَإِذِ ٱعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ فَأْوُۥٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُم مِّنْ أَمْرِكُم مِّرْفَقًۭا
items.verseEn: [The youths said to one another], "And when you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship other than Allāh, retreat to the cave. Your Lord will spread out for you of His mercy and will prepare for you from your affair facility."

items.verse: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَعْثَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّۭ وَأَنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَآ إِذْ يَتَنَـٰزَعُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَمْرَهُمْ ۖ فَقَالُوا۟ ٱبْنُوا۟ عَلَيْهِم بُنْيَـٰنًۭا ۖ رَّبُّهُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِهِمْ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ غَلَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرِهِمْ لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ عَلَيْهِم مَّسْجِدًۭا
items.verseEn: And similarly, We caused them to be found that they [who found them] would know that the promise of Allāh is truth and that of the Hour there is no doubt. [That was] when they disputed among themselves about their affair and [then] said, "Construct over them a structure. Their Lord is most knowing about them." Said those who prevailed in the matter, "We will surely take [for ourselves] over them a masjid."

categories.name: أَفْعَالٌ تَصِفُ حَالَاتِ الْقَلْبِ أَوِ الْجَسَدِ
categories.nameEn: Verbs describing states or reactions of the heart or body

items.verse: ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَجِلَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَصَابَهُمْ وَٱلْمُقِيمِى ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ
items.verseEn: Who, when Allāh is mentioned, their hearts are fearful, and [to] the patient over what has afflicted them, and the establishers of prayer and those who spend from what We have provided them.

items.verse: وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَـٰقَكُمْ وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ ٱلطُّورَ خُذُوا۟ مَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰكُم بِقُوَّةٍۢ وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ ۖ قَالُوا۟ سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا وَأُشْرِبُوا۟ فِى قُلُوبِهِمُ ٱلْعِجْلَ بِكُفْرِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ بِئْسَمَا يَأْمُرُكُم بِهِۦٓ إِيمَـٰنُكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
items.verseEn: And [recall] when We took your covenant and raised over you the mount, [saying], "Take what We have given you with determination and listen." They said [instead], "We hear and disobey." And their hearts absorbed [the worship of] the calf because of their disbelief. Say, "How wretched is that which your faith enjoins upon you, if you should be believers."

items.verse: وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ ٱشْمَأَزَّتْ قُلُوبُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَإِذَا ذُكِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِذَا هُمْ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ
items.verseEn: And when Allāh is mentioned alone, the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter shrink with aversion, but when those [worshipped] other than Him are mentioned, immediately they rejoice.

items.verse: ٱللَّهُ نَزَّلَ أَحْسَنَ ٱلْحَدِيثِ كِتَـٰبًۭا مُّتَشَـٰبِهًۭا مَّثَانِىَ تَقْشَعِرُّ مِنْهُ جُلُودُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَخْشَوْنَ رَبَّهُمْ ثُمَّ تَلِينُ جُلُودُهُمْ وَقُلُوبُهُمْ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ يَهْدِى بِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍ
items.verseEn: Allāh has sent down the best statement: a consistent Book wherein is reiteration. The skins shiver therefrom of those who fear their Lord; then their skins and their hearts relax at the remembrance [i.e., mention] of Allāh. That is the guidance of Allāh by which He guides whom He wills. And one whom Allāh sends astray - for him there is no guide.

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ قِصَّةِ أَصْحَابِ الْجَنَّةِ
categories.nameEn: Words from the story of the owners of the garden (Surah Al-Qalam)

items.en: that they would surely harvest it
items.verse: إِنَّا بَلَوْنَـٰهُمْ كَمَا بَلَوْنَآ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا۟ لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ
items.verseEn: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning
So they set out, while lowering their voices,
And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.

items.verse: قَالَ أَوْسَطُهُمْ أَلَمْ أَقُل لَّكُمْ لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
items.verseEn: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allāh]?'"

categories.name: كَلِمَاتٌ مِنْ حِوَارِ اللَّهِ مَعَ إِبْلِيسَ
categories.nameEn: Words from the Quranic dialogues between Allah and Iblis

items.verse: قَالَ فَٱهْبِطْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُونُ لَكَ أَن تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيهَا فَٱخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰغِرِينَ
items.verseEn: [Allāh] said, "Descend from it [i.e., Paradise], for it is not for you to be arrogant therein. So get out; indeed, you are of the debased."

items.verse: قَالَ فَبِمَآ أَغْوَيْتَنِى لَأَقْعُدَنَّ لَهُمْ صِرَٰطَكَ ٱلْمُسْتَقِيمَ
items.verseEn: [Satan] said, "Because You have put me in error, I will surely sit in wait for them [i.e., mankind] on Your straight path.

items.verse: قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرْنِىٓ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ
items.verseEn: He said, "My Lord, then reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected."
[Allāh] said, "This is a path [of return] to Me [that is] straight.

harf.hint: A name for the eternal gardens of paradise.
harf.verse: 38:50 — Gardens of perpetual residence, whose doors will be opened to them.

deduction.title: The Verdict of the Fields
deduction.intro: You are presented with a case about a powerful leader whose wisdom was divinely inspired. This individual was granted dominion over forces unseen by most, commanding armies of different species and understanding languages beyond human comprehension. Their reign was marked by justice, immense power, and a profound submission to the One who granted it all. Can you identify this remarkable servant of Allah?
clues: I was blessed with immense wisdom, even as a youth, allowing me to deliver a judgment that surpassed that of my own father.
I inherited not just a kingdom, but prophethood as well.
I could command the wind, directing it as I willed by the permission of my Lord.
My armies consisted not only of humans, but also of jinn and birds, all marshaled in their ranks.
I understood the speech of an ant, and its warning to its colony caused me to smile in amusement.
A hoopoe bird brought me news of a distant, sun-worshipping people, prompting me to send them a message of guidance.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Sulayman
Prophet Dhul-Kifl
Prophet Yusuf
Dhul-Qarnayn
Prophet Dawud

trial.label: Defining Event
options: Receiving news from a hoopoe about a sun-worshipping kingdom
Being swallowed by a giant fish as a test
Building an ark to save the believers from a great flood
Splitting the sea with a staff to escape a tyrant
Being thrown into a fire for rejecting idols
trial.answer: Receiving news from a hoopoe about a sun-worshipping kingdom

location.label: Key Element
options: The Valley of the Ants
A preserved body on the seashore
A golden calf built for worship
A shirt stained with false blood
A she-camel emerging from a rock

outcome.label: Significant Outcome
options: The Queen of Sheba submitted to Allah along with me
My people were destroyed by a mighty wind for their disbelief
I was raised to a high place after my people plotted against me
Allah caused me to die for a hundred years and then resurrected me
I was given a book of wisdom to teach my son about shirk
outcome.answer: The Queen of Sheba submitted to Allah along with me

deduction.arabic: وَحُشِرَ لِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ جُنُودُهُۥ مِنَ ٱلْجِنِّ وَٱلْإِنسِ وَٱلطَّيْرِ فَهُمْ يُوزَعُونَ
deduction.verse: And gathered for Solomon were his soldiers of the jinn and men and birds, and they were [marching] in rows (27:17)

translations: What (was) restored (by) Allah to His Messenger from (the) people (of) the towns (it is) for Allah and His Messenger
and for those (of) the kindred and the orphans and the needy and the wayfarer that not it becomes
a (perpetual) circulation between the rich among you And whatever gives you the Messenger take it and whatever
he forbids you from it refrain And fear Allah Indeed Allah (is) severe (in) penalty
scramble.hint: This verse establishes a fundamental principle for Muslims: obedience to the Prophet's commands and prohibitions.
scramble.arabic: مَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ وَلِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ كَىْ لَا يَكُونَ دُولَةًۢ بَيْنَ ٱلْأَغْنِيَآءِ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَمَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمُ ٱلرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ فَٱنتَهُوا۟ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ
words: مَّآ أَفَآءَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِۦ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ فَلِلَّهِ وَلِلرَّسُولِ
وَلِذِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ وَٱلْيَتَـٰمَىٰ وَٱلْمَسَـٰكِينِ وَٱبْنِ ٱلسَّبِيلِ كَىْ لَا يَكُونَ
دُولَةًۢ بَيْنَ ٱلْأَغْنِيَآءِ مِنكُمْ ۚ وَمَآ ءَاتَىٰكُمُ ٱلرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا
نَهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ فَٱنتَهُوا۟ ۚ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ
scramble.verseEn: And what Allāh restored to His Messenger from the people of the towns - it is for Allāh and for the Messenger and for [his] near relatives and orphans and the needy and the [stranded] traveler - so that it will not be a perpetual distribution among the rich from among you. And whatever the Messenger has given you - take; and what he has forbidden you - refrain from. And fear Allāh; indeed, Allāh is severe in penalty.
